title: Header Fields, Media Types, Negotiation, JSON, and Runtime Validation · Browser and Node Client Systems · Contract Defense
summary: A cross-runtime release reviewer evaluating a changed provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for header fields, media types, negotiation, json, and runtime validation, producing representation negotiation and validation boundary. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch representation negotiation and validation boundary. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use Content-Type for enclosed content and Accept for preferred response media ranges with explicit fallback. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Distinguish representation content coding, message delimitation, content length, and decoded size. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Field Semantics · changed case · Quiz Field Semantics
steps.instruction:
quiz case — universal Fetch release candidate asks for application/json and receives Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Encoding gzip, Vary Accept-Language, and field revision:6. Distinguish metadata layers, bound decoded bytes, and reject a changed value type. Competency probe: Handle field names, repeated lines, combination rules, whitespace, size, and hop-by-hop boundaries by specification. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

For Fetch and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Language Charset Negotiation, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: JSON, text, bytes, Unicode code points, and user-perceived characters are interchangeable. This remains false for TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch.
